text: I usually use white or black binders and find the purple of the [[ASIN:B00CPXG220 Wilson Jones Heavy Duty Round Ring View Binder with Extra Durable Hinge, 1-Inch, Purple (W363-14-267)]] to be attractive, impressive and be easier to notice.  The quality of the binder is average and in my experience, it's not any better than others I've used throughout the years.  It's actually lighter weight than I was expecting and there is a bit of puckering in the back grommet area.  The binder lines up well and otherwise appears to be well made but I think it's quite expensive for a single binder.  Nevertheless, the color ups it a star in my review and I'm glad to  have it for my needed purpose.
Only two colors, the orange and the green, have strong enough contrast to be usable.
The wheels themselves are great, but that swivel bearing race needs a better design.

Way much better than I had expected,  the Smead stick-n-store packets are just what I needed to keep those little receipts, cards and papers in order inside files.  They are sturdy but not weighty,  have functional zippers and metal grommets at both ends of the zipper. Sticky strip world fine and as long as I don't overfill the packet,  it is perfect.
Guzzles ink. Didn't last very long do to extended us.
Not sturdy and confusing to assemble. Very cheap material. If you're looking for a solid file cabinet, I would look elsewhere. This is made in China, and it shows.

It's been many years since I've sold on eBay, but do occasionally need to send a package or large envelope and a receipt.  For those times, the Avery shipping label with receipt all on one single page is a convenient supply to have on hand.  I can use the usps, paypal, ebay or stamps.com sites to print these and include the postage in the stamp or I can use what I have designed pretty quickly using word.  These are not as cheap as separate address labels and receipts printed on plain paper, but you get what you pay for with these.  A professional impression is created and if you recycle packaging materials, the label does cover up previous labels or ink very well.  I like the two part label/receipt option for occasional, so this package of 50 is great for my needs and pocketbook.
Great concept but they don’t stay adhered to the boxes.  You will have to tape them down.

We have owned both the Epson Artisan 800 and the artisan 835. Both printers has terrible paper handling and uses lots ink even when not printing. The average cost per page is over $3, since the ink so so expensive and the machine is useless after a couple of years, when the ink pads are full, which shuts down the machine entirely and none of the other functions work. Epson's rather shabby customer service solution is to buy another printer.

Nice looking printer, which prints CD's which is a big plus for us, however I would NOT recommend this printer, due to the extremely high cost of printing.

Five Star offers a wide assortment of products for the locker and they typically are fine for a school year.  This one is small and not perfectly balanced.  Having about a third of the space for the push pin area would be more sensible because that would provide more white board space for writing.  The little enclosed magnets were basically fused to mine in shipping and they have left a permanent impression on the board. The color red leans toward the pink red aRea of the color spectrum.
Nice, looks great but everything except for large papers and envelopes fall thru the wire frame

I ordered a Palm Z22 in April. I was not overly impressed but it did suit my needs.  Barely a month of owning it, it quit working.  I could not get it to turn on.  Someone else that had one said she had a similar problem in that it stopped working (coming on) as well.  She left her palm on her dresser for a few months.  One day while she was cleaning up she came across her Z22.  She was about to throw it out but decided to try it one more time and it came on. She finally gave up on it when it did it again a month or so later.  So I tried that --- it still does not work.  I would not recommend this product to anyone.  That was $95 down the drain.

Mont Blanc doesn't offer a huge selection of ink colors, so if you're determined to get a red MB ink, the [[ASIN:B008SBO7CK Montblanc Burgundy Red New Ink Cartridge 2010 Refill]] is your choice.  It's also available in the lovely Mont Blanc bottle, [[ASIN:B003M5T3XA Genuine Montblanc Bottled Ink Refill - Burgundy Red 105198]], which is a better value if your pen has a converter or you're comfortable performing the simple task of refilling your cartridge.  I was expecting the color to be as described, "For expression Burgundy Red blends rounded wine tones with flaming fire for intense passionate language."  In reality, when I compared this ink in my [[ASIN:B00C4180LO Mont Blanc Meisterstuck Mozart Platinum Line Mini Fountain Pen 107706]] and a [[ASIN:B00EVC948C Pilot MR Animal Collection Fountain Pen, Matte Gold with Lizard Accent, Medium Point, 91136]] containing [[ASIN:B0013D2XYY Private Reserve Black Cherry Ink Bottle 50 ML]], my impression was that the color is nearly as brown as the PR.  It is a little more red, but there's nto a huge difference between the two.  Nevertheless, the cartridges work as expected in Mont Blanc fountain pens.  I purchased mine in a pen store for a substantial savings over the current prices.
Just ok. Not as big as described. Plastic smells.

I had high hopes for this, imagining that the monthly white boards would be solid affairs on smooth runners that could simply be shuffled in order easily. In fact, this is a bog standard 3'x2' white board with a shelf that also has three small flimsy 1'x2' card inserts for the 3 months that you have to manually pull out a reorganize (they have a plasticized covering to make them wipeable). As a final disappointment it comes with a pack of large tip dry erase markers -- how on earth is anyone supposed to use a large tip dry erase marker on this when each day of the month only has a 1.8"x2.9" area?
(this was ordered from work which is why I'm not a verified purchaser)
Have owned for 7 months now. Plastic braces on lower back digs into my spine. Had to add a pad for comfort. Tilt back function failed after 6 months.
To large for a key chain and not returnable.
There is no color accuracy with these inks. If your not printing out photos, save your money and buy these inks. If you are printing photos, stick with the Epson brand inks.

The shape of this eraser fits more comfortably in the hand than a traditional blocky style of chalkboard or whiteboard eraser.  Much more comfortably,  and the ends of it can be used in crevices with ease,  so I can have better access into areas inside the edge of a frame.  With two different surfaces on one removable section,  I can use the one I need,  then reverse it when I need the other side.  There is also a section with a piece of blue microfiber fabric which we all are familiar with,  the kind we use for washing,  drying and polishing our cars,  homes, boats and pet's faces and paws. Keep the old ratty ones with holes or stains because they can be cut to the shape of this eraser and stuck to the textured area under the existing microfiber.  I expect the one included to eventually ravel away at the pointed ends because of their narrow form.  This is a very smart,  space saving little tool for home or work.

Box came today, no damage to box, BUT inside one of the arms was bent, and it appeared before it was painted, the plastic cover by the wheel is damaged, STATED ON box, it was shipped from Windsor VA, to Amazon logistics, Carlisle Pa.

Quality Control in my opinion did not happen, or it would not have been shipped.

I was planning on buying two more, but will have to weigh the reward or do I just repair it like we did today.

Would not purchase another one, since the chairs only fit on the bottom rungs, Given two stars only, have not heard from anyone

Years ago, Trapper Keeper products were heavy duty and lasted for years.  Kids tended to decorate them because they were pretty basic in designs.  The Star Wars Trapper Keeper is much more expensive than any of those were and it is simply a binder with a closable front flap.  Because it is very  flimsy, I do not expect it to last beyond a single school year.

Made in China, the [[ASIN:B00KSEAID2 Mead Academie Watercolor Paint Set, 12 Pans, Assorted (98024)]] comes in a white metal tin case and is a good size for portability.  Each of the dozen colors gives an immediate bland impression when compared to other paints geared to adults, but for transparents, they are adequate.  Nicer than the cheaper Prang paints we used when I taught Middle School Art, I would consider these a step or two up the ladder.  Students might experiment with cosmetics and make-up initially, while as they mature, they likely will discern characteristics and qualities as they gain experience.  The same holds true for artists and their colors.  Because these are translucent, the colors are not vibrant or rich, but they can be blended with others to create lovely effects.  The included brush is very nice although the one in my case is a bit warped.  If I were getting this for a new artist, I'd be certain to include other brushes because this has a fine tip and I think they could benefit from broader versions as well.  The brush is very soft.  It is a nice set of water-soluble watercolours [sic].
Cheap. Have to use two bands. They break and snap easily.  I  can get sturdier ones and use less. Good if you are in a pinch.

Have had the 3570c for 4 years, connected to my mac. It's always been marginal, and lately virtually useless--even after reloading scanner software numerous times and troubleshooting. The software is horrible and extremely unstable.

Love new model of vanishing point pen. Color is luscious. I chose the extra fine point nib which is not as smooth as the fine or medium point, but since I already had those points, I chose the extra fine.
CAME TO ME ALREADY DRIED OUT.
Not sure how the design could be improved, but we had trouble fastening these to different styles of clothing.  I think for durable nametags the lanyard style is more satisfactory.

Made in the USA, the [[ASIN:B00D8GK904 Intertape Polymer Group TS2650 Bundle Wrap Stretch Film, 2-Inch x 650-Feet]] is certainly expensive, but it definitely sticks to itself, just like it's supposed to.  It is wonderful for bundling items for mailing or storing, but it is also perfect for stretching around a bottle top, sealing it to itself for security without adhesive.  It's not for a full seal or pretty presentation, but it is an added security if you are shipping things like a bottle of homemade vanilla extract and you want to provide an extra level of leakage protection.  It is 120 gauge and very stretchy, much more than a saran wrap type of plastic, and it is easy to tear with the hand.
Rather over priced for what you get, but you live and learn. It's nice that the magnet isn't attached as then you can put it where ever you want.
This was so sticky it was really hard to get off of the backing and it made a mess. I think it will be very difficult to remove later.

A pack of 100 7 Mil Fellowes Ultra Clear PET Binding Covers in the Letter Size can be used not only in a binding system but are easy to cut by hand for other purposes needing sturdy sheets of plastic.  After binding some documents with these as front and back covers, I needed to find something for packaging earrings I'd made.  I have been repurposing lightweight cardboard stock from coffee shops, then was inspired to try incorporating pieces of this plastic in the packaging.  It works extremely well and provides a substantial base for Sharpie markings (which must be allowed to dry or they will rub off), holes or cutouts, and other hand stitched or cemented embellishments.  I had no idea when I requested these binding covers that I'd be going through the box of 100 so quickly.  Packaged in a perfectly sized white box with a flip top, it's easy to remove each sheet without messing up those which remain in the box.  They are separated with tissue paper which I've also reused.

I bought this about a year ago about. I am happy with it few things would change the paper itself should be decorative also coming with pencil or pen. Also, it would be nice if it comes in packs of 2 or something extra for the price.
Besides this so far happy with it

Two of six badge holders came damaged, already torn at the top edge. I bought these to replace me old badger holder that has also torn in the same place, top edge. My old badge holder has held up for over a year. These are brand new and already torn 👎🏼

Had I seen this card in a store, it would not have made a good impression. The paper of which the card is constructed -- not a very good quality, despite being sustainably sourced and made in the USA, lends itself to bending where your don't want it to bend. The face of the card does not make as good an impression as does a digital photo image, and because of the less than stellar paper fibers, I am using ballpoint pen rather than gel ink or a fountain pen.

I recently got a standing desk with a white glass top and have found that the ability to write directly on the glass with dry erase markets was a time and paper saver. It's so easy to wipe off the writing with the sweep of a tissue or micro fiber cloth and keep working on a clean desk. This easel life's exactly the same capabilities in a small format and I'm finding it perfect in the kitchen. The secure clip can be attached to any edge, I use it on the top for most cars or don't use it at all of I need the entire surface with no paper being held. I can print a recipe, clip it on the glass easel and make my notes on the board. Entree the notes into the computer when I can, then wipe them away and have a pristine glass easel. I also use this for taking notes during phone calls and then transcribe them later. It's saved me a lot of paper lately in my numerous conversations with Verizon Fios. It was comforting to sit in an oversized chair with this on my lap as I wrote my notes with this in the closed position. The plastic backing is rigid but does not add much weight. I am not a big fan of the enclosed marker so I use my preferred brand with darker ink instead.
needs a clear cover. It doesn't fit into a clear cover that I have used for several years.

In my experience, any dry erase markers do not last long, they tend to dry out if not used often.  I've also found that most have been of the low odor ink formula, and although I don't know for a fact, perhaps this is a reason they do not remain moist.  Nevertheless, I really like these pens for several reasons.  The rubberized grip is definitely easy on the fingers and if my hand is wet with water or moisturizer, I can keep a hold of the marker.  The point is what is called fine, but don't confuse this with the mush finer tips as you would find in a permanent marker.  The ink is solid, flow is even when the pen is new, the cap stays on when snapped on with a little pressure, and I've been able to rig up a hanger attached to the pocket clip of the cap which I've hung next to the mirror at our front door.  I can write directly on the glass face of the mirror and erase it with a tissue or fabric as required.  It's a good option for those who need to write on white boards, glass or mirrors, and I appreciate that they were made in the USA, even if some of the materials have been outsourced.
It prints poorly and less clarity colors and looks really cheap.

I purchased these due to the positive though sparse reviews.  On the plus side, the colors are very pretty.  But that's about the only positive thing I can say about them.  1) only the silver and gold look metallic; 2) after one use, the blue one completely ran dry; 3) surface absorption was poor resulting in the ink coming off on my hands even 30 minutes after application, so I had to use a coat of varnish to seal them.  I can't recommend them.

I ordered a more expensive footrest because of the features offered.  It does not work very well, and has a cheap plastic feel to it.e  The first time I tried to adjust it, one of the nuts fell off into the inside of the bottom tray, and I cannot retrieve it.  I had to place a box under one side to keep it level.  It was a great design idea that did not get treated well in the final product.  Not worth the money I paid for it.  Would have done just as well with a stationary footrest.

I'm a big fan of paper made in France so I took a chance with a team of the Amazon Basics copy paper. The cost team was sort of banged up when it arrived but the replacement ream was in good shape. It is indeed white paper, paper thin sheets (pun intended) which are smooth to the touch. This is why I love paper made in France. Printing is fine, writing on it is fine, cutting or tearing it is fine, and it can be folded into soaring airplane forms.
This is a great pencil/pen holder. Good looking and made well. Makes a great gift!

The AmazonBasics 17-Sheet Cross-Cut Paper/CD/Credit Card Shredder with Pullout Basket is a workhorse of a shredder and it weighs a ton.  Fortunately, it arrives with attachable rolling wheels (be sure you insert the locking pair in the front of the unit) because it otherwise would be better suited for a designated spot.  On the wheels, it can be moved from room to room as needed, so if you have the need for mobility, this is for you.  The machine is very intuitive and the manual is quite simple to follow, too.  I first shredded an old users manual but fed it into the shredder vertically, so I was able to read the little slices of paper in the bin.  This did stress to me the importance of feeding the sheets through just as they are read, thereby resulting in slices which are more difficult to reassemble.  I have another shredder which produces little confetti pieces, but that one has always been a struggle to operate. These strips are longer, but the machine works much better.  It is suggested to use it for no longer than nine minutes at a time, but since I seldom need to do that much shredding, it's not an issue here.  Of important note is that when shredding CDs or credit cards, be sure to insert them in the right side of the shredder.  It is clearly marked, but I know what it's like when I'm on auto pilot and don't think or observe, just do.  With a very large basket, it's not necessary to empty the paper as often as in smaller models which would likely compete in this price range.  I insert a bag in mine so that when the time comes, I have little to clean out.  Most does remain in the bag.  The manual makes no recommendation to oil the shredder, but since I have been in the habit of routinely oiling my previous models, I'll likely do the same with this one unless I learn that it's not suggested.  All in all, I love this shredder.
Nice ringer. Hard to set up but WiFi is great
Like the message,  this card is more elegant than many others in the line. The complementary color scheme is gorgeous and the paper feels good. It will make a great impression.
It's flimsy when you first get it, and it just gets flimsier and flimsier until it's basically completely useless. All within a month.
Does not work. Waste of money. DO NOT BUY

This ink runs out so fast! I feel like I print 5 pages and have to put a new one in. The printer was cheap like $100 I guess that’s how they get you, on the ink purchases. I’d suggest not getting the HP envy 4512 it apparently drinks black ink like it’s free
It was dull. Initially, I thought the plastic covering was still on, one removed it was just as dull, worn and old. I returned it.
each piece you cut turns inside itself into a circle. it does not look like the picture. i received two verywide tall circles with  very thin tape.
These are very pretty envelopes! Just what I needed!
I am so frustrated with this printer. Really regret buying it. Would not recommend it.
No no no! These do not look like the picture. The colors are not bright, or metallic or holographic. Dull and lighter. Nice paper weight and verse inside. Very disappointed.
This thing drives me crazy. Since it’s hollow, the noise it makes as I walk across a quiet office building seems to bounce around everywhere. Everyone can hear me coming.

I read many reviews and should have paid attention to those saying the company cut back on the sizing.
It stinks because I have been using these for over 15yrs. without a problem and now have to worry about something else to take up my time.
Unfortunately, I missed the return window, however about 80% of the markers were dried out.  I wished I'd read the reviews before purchasing.
Not all blue inks have the same blue color. I was pleasantly surprised with the color of this blue ink.

I am a big fan of anything that introduces young persons to art and Faber Castell has hit it out of the ballpark with the quite complete set which also introduces them to color.  Let's face it, too often kids want to play with makeup, not realizing they are exploring texture and color, and at a price that rivals makeup marketed to kids (notably girl targeted), they can experience the texture and color on paper.  They even receive little vials not unlike those we can get at department stores which contain cosmetics samples, yet the kids can create their own shades and use them later, then wash out the vial and do it again.  A mortar and pestle helps them learn what happens when colors are blended together into powder form and see what happens as water is added, thereby learning about hues and shades.  This includes brushes and paper to get them started.  I do have to say that this is a suitable set for adults as well as children, and would be a really nice addition to an adult day care facility for producing art and creative expression.
It’s does not work on wood, maybe work on something soft, but on wood panel doesn’t work
It's lighter and thinner than I expected. It didn't fit my needs and I gave it to my daughter and she's using it on her desk.
i like them
I do recommend buying these.  Of course, for each person it can be different when I use them paper clips can stack about 7 sheets together.
The print does not dry well after printing on the labels.
Highlights just fine, but DOES NOT ERASE 🙄

This item is poorly made, it wouldn't snap in properly into its slot in the printer, I had to to tape it to the other inks and once it was pushed down, I was able to print, the plastic part toward the front broke off immediately. I ordered my other inks on amazon, and never had this "fit"problem. Maybe this one was just defective, but I won't order from the same seller again

These are not schlage door stops, these are a cheap imitation.  Schlage do not have casting seams, and they have a flathead bit at the end (under the rubber cap) that allows you to use a screwdriver to install them.  These are terrible and I feel ripped off.
Nothe bad for a seat.Help my low back size.

Our problem with the mat is we were looking for a chair mat that wouldn't shift on our carpet. We measured the carpet pile and ordered the mat that was supposed to work for that depth of carpet. It shifts! We were disappointed. We plan to order it again with deeper teeth in hopes of getting one with that won't shift.

We have moved this mat to a different place and find while it still shifts, it moves less than the other mats we've used. We reordered it in a heavier weight and while it still moves, it is satisfactory.

Just as expected,  the black vinyl letters are positioned on their sheets to maximize space and reduce waste of black vinyl. Using fingernails or a small sharp tool,  each letter can be lifted for use without destroying adjacent letters. Be sure to check the amounts of letters and symbols prior to perchance or you may require more than one package. My project needed only one (to my surprise), so I was able to complete it rather quickly after lining them up.  I tested removal of these by using extras and they were removable after a few days without leaving residue.
Good

Many of these markers are dried out. However I need them for my daughter in laws baby shower in 3 days [guests will be decorating wooden blocks] so I do not have time to send them back and was it for a replacement. I have to use what will work and purchase additional markers to supplement. Very disappointing, and ghosting more money at the last minute.
flimsy cardboard

The Rhodia Notepads Graph Black Wb 8.3 x 11.7 is a dream to write on.  The large dimensions allow it to be held comfortably on the lap, so a table or desk aren't required as often is the case with smaller pads of paper.  The paper is white, the grids are gray, and they are perfectly printed.  With micro perforations at the top of the page, removal is simple.  I just received two pads and both are interesting in that the wire binding on both pads is finished with all four ends of the wire is turned in toward the center of the page.  Imagine that they have been pushed inward so as to secure the spiral wires.  I didn't notice this in the pads I checked out at a pen store prior to my purchase, but both of these pads share the characteristic, so there you have it.  I have used mechanical pencil, rollerball, fiber tip and fountain pens on the paper and each works well.  Because the paper is so smooth and substantial in weight, even with a fountain pen using dark black ink, I am able to flip a sheet over and continue writing on the reverse side.  Therefore, on an 80 sheet pad, I have 160 pages which to cover with ink or graphite.  I prefer the wire bound pads to the stapled pads because of the ease with which I can have a single sheet visible and I find the pad to be sturdy enough to require no other backing.  Fountain pen nibs move more smoothly on this paper than most others I've used lately, and I am reminded why I first bought a pad of Rhodia paper in France many, many years ago.  The graph option is my favorite but I'm likely to try the dot next.  My only criticism of purchasing this from Amazon is that a sticker is applied to the back of the pad and it's annoying to work to fully remove the residue. It took a lot of rubbing because I didn't want to apply anything even though the covers are water resistant.  Be aware that your pad of paper will likely not remain pristine for long.  These are made to be filled and that means they will be touched, often moved from place to place.  They don't hold up like a bound journal will because they are simple bound sheets of paper in a thin front and back cover with a cardboard sheet inserted at the back of the sheets of paper for support.
Nice little planner. Has a capture of the month before the month tab and boxes on each day with several writing lines.
So far the times I have used these I am happy with it when I give them to others they look to be pleased. Happy.

I return a lot of packages and love this tape.

Update: The latest version of this is much thinner, harder to use and very disappointing!
Firm and sturdy...just keep away from water, obviously.
only going to buy from the store now or straight from the manufacturer

My first impression of the [[ASIN:B00GDDV5UW Mead Organizher School Memory Keeper, 14-Folder Organizher, 10.25 x 11.5 Inches, Purple (64051)]] was unfavorable. It looked cheap and unattractive. I thought that it couldn't possibly hold everything a memory keeper should hold. After looking at it for several days, touching it a few times, today I did a test run by filling the folders with pamphlets and catalogues to see how much it would hold. To my surprise, the binder holds a substantial amount and the elastic band which is bolted into the back cover stretches amply to keep the binder, closing tightly. This binder isn't waterproof or protective at all, but it's fine for keeping things that don't need to be kept indefinitely. We could always keep the binder inside a vacuum sealed bag for long term protection, then take it out annually, I guess, but I find a plastic sealed box to be better. My compromise is that this will be kept inside the plastic box instead of on a shelf. We will digitally retain all things by scanning or photographing, but the tangible items are what we really want for memories. Of the sections designated on the back of each folder, I think my favorite line is the one for a signature. I would like to see how that changes over fourteen years time. This memory keeper apparently is currently available in a purple or gray, neither of which I think is beautiful nor memorable. I'm not a fan of the Mead name for this line, "Organizher," and had I not thought it was a listing typo, I likely would not have requested this item.

This is better than average but not a fabulous product. I personally prefer using three ringed binders with available sleeves that do the same as the ones in this product. I like the option of removing the pages and adding another binder when necessary and prefer not paying fifteen dollars for a matching binder, if it will still be available in years to come. We tend to keep a lot of memories which we shuffle through over time, so this isn't a great product for our family, but it might be a good product for those who like to maintain a more orderly organizer. My sister likes to keep things like this standardized and displayed on a shelf, so she would have loved these for her three kids. Although the quality isn't as high, this reminds me of leather bound photo albums she had lining a bookshelf for her kids so I may buy her a couple of these for her grandchildren. She won't have as many things to fill the pages and one will be designated for each child. I think it would be better for grandparents than parents who collect a lot of their kids' creations and accolades.
Sometimes charges would go through, sometimes not . Fits loosely in phone jack. Hard to swipe cards. Undependable.
Terrible quality, and pens came barely with any ink.

Setting up the[[ASIN:B005IVL0RS Epson WorkForce 845 Wireless Color Printer (C11CB92201)]] was not difficult once I'd downloaded the software onto my Mac running Snow Leopard.  Printing while wired was seamless, fast, quieter than I'd expected for such a large printer, and I anticipated the wireless operation to be just as simple.  The quality of printing was superb for an inkjet printer and I couldn't wait to try it out on a network.  Unfortunately, this task was not as easy but after several attempts and phone calls, I'm finally up and running, quite happy that I can now even print from my smartphone.  Because the included cartridges are full size, I haven't yet ordered replacement inks, but will once again return to Amazon for those purchases.  It's been some years since I owned an Epson printer, but this one is now certainly utilized in my home office, sending the little Canon printer into the closet for emergencies only and putting the Brother MF on hiatus.  The Epson is much easier to operate, produces a better quality print job and has a more legible screen.  I definitely recommend this printer and suggest that if you're using a Mac, don't be discouraged.  It's worth the effort.

Bought this for my epson xp 434. The vlank in shows full but won't work. All I get is blank papers. I tried both black inks they both are not good. The color ones worked but barely. Color is really faded . not happy with this purchase. Save your money and order from somewhere else.
Ink bots and skips. Not a good purchase.

For our Fellowes shredder, this was a necessity.  It's oil and it's manufactured for use in shredders, and it has the Fellowes brand name behind it.  After shredding a "ton" of documents, I realized how important it was to periodically apply drops of the oil to a sheet of paper and feed it through the shredder to keep it moving.  I now have another printer which doesn't require the oil, so the bottle which has a desirable extended nozzle will go with the old shredder to a good friend.  I will also let them know the importance of lubricating the blades!  16 ounces will last much longer than most homes need, but for an office setting, it's perfect.

This item comes in a 3-pack.  One is only needed for one door to keep from slamming shut on a windy day.  As for another door, there is more space from the floor.  I tried to use two stacked as demo'd by video however, they do not stay together so, I don't use them for that particular door.  Otherwise, the price is right.

I bought this 'heavy duty' stapler thinking I could put price tags on my wooden (pine) craft items.  It won't staple paper to wood, even tho the paper is thin and the wood is soft.  Also, when you open the stapler to add more staples, you get poked from the 'next in line' staple that is sticking out.  Won't buy again or even recommend this 'heavy duty' stapler to anyone.

Thourougly disappointing product. I have ordered products like this for many years. This is not well wrapped on the tube and constantly get me stuck trying to unroll. Many others out there with superior and cheaper wrapping plastic.
I like the fact that it only has 6 tapes.  But it doesn't stick at all, I ended up returned it.

Use them to band stacks of currency and then put in office safe.  Found several bands on the top stacks had come loose and bills scattered when I grabbed the money.  Only works well when the bands have heavy pressure levied on them.

Everyone loves a door prize, but no one like to spend a lot of time filling our their tickets with name and info.  The save time, the numbers are easy to read and people want to win so they pay attention.  Good bargain, I recommend.

I bought these to give my AP Language & Composition students on the day I assigned the Rhetorical Analysis prompt excerpted Jennifer Price’s essay “The Plastic Pink Flamingo: A Natural History.”

These are 11th grade students, and they loved it — almost everyone used their. We pen to write their timed essay that day.

With close to a month of usage, the Samsung SL-C1860FW/XAA Wireless Color Printer with Scanner, Copier and Fax is providing a much appreciated upgrade to my home office. Its larger than I'd expected, but I had not researched its size prior to requesting the machine. The box I received was intact and it required two of us to remove the product from the package. Setting it up was problematic at first because there was no CD to be found anywhere. After I'd downloaded the manual from the Samsung website, I finally found the CD hidden inside the box, so if you purchase this and can't find the CD, keep looking. Enclosed in clear sight was a warranty and two folded pages with installation information. I found them to be convoluted, but that could be my problem. I also had difficulty connecting this to my network, and finally was able to connect wirelessly. I honestly do not know the steps I took, but it does work and it's securely a part of my own network. The cartridges are easy and quick to install, as is the paper tray. I was apprehensive with the tray because it was more lightweight than those on the enormous laser printers I used in days past. Visually, this machine is attractive and boxy, neutral in colors and the screen is very intuitive and clear. Although not required per the documentation, I downloaded the Samsung software on our smartphones and we are not using this printer in this manner rather than using Google Cloud. I've not attempted to use the NFC Print via the one smartphone with capabilities only because I haven't needed that yet.

Copying is fast and accurate, crisp and clean. Scans are fantastic and reliable. The fax I tested was successful. Printing was equally successful when sent via USB or WiFi. The option to print configurations is appreciated, as that allows for keeping all information in a folder kept near the printer. I use the Eco mode because I don't use the machine every day. I haven't notice warmth around the machine and it is quiet when not functional. For my home office, the speed of printing is great, and although I likely will not use this for as much dedicated color printing as my other inkjet printers, for documents which include color, this is better than my other printers. The major weakness for my needs is that this does not have duplex printing. I have yet to experience a paper jam. I'm far from needing replacement toners, but hope that their prices are reduced before that day comes. My initial thought regarding the cost of this machine was that it's priced higher than it is worth, but considering the cost of the stuff that goes onto the paper is about the same as this entire package, I guess I won't complain. If you are in the market for a very good color multifunctional printer, this is definitely worth considering.

Having received three of these Avery Durable clear cover binders over the past few weeks, I can no longer remain silent. Each one has a pocket inside the cover and each one has a pocket which has split. The exterior looks good, particularly when personalized sheets of paper have been inserted into the cover sleeves, each one has perfectly functioning slant rings, each one holds the tabbed dividers perfectly, but the interior pockets look as though they were sliced with a knife. These are very fine for home purposes, but I would not consider them for distribution of materials requiring more than occasional use and would hesitate before using them for student purposes which require daily use.

The hardware is excellent in these binders and they are attached well into the rear of the binder so the sturdiness is increased, I just do not like the flimsy plastic parts of the pockets which are terrible.

My only criticism of the Avery full sheets sticker paper in beautiful gold is that the backing could use more slices to allow for quicker and cleaner removal after smaller sections are printed.  I think that most people will cut these either by mechanical or scissor/blade methods, and if you make smaller stickers as I've done, you may find that some must be peeled from the edge.  The paper is thick, so with care, destruction can be avoided, and if you are not a perfectionist, you might not mind anyway.  Since I often have problems with Avery templates printing not so perfectly onto precut shapes, I'm more satisfied by cutting my own with a blade after printing.  I've been having fun with these and appreciate the exposure to a full sized sheet of sticker paper.

I ordered this before beginning my first job as a teacher. I finished grad school in December and was hired for a mid-year opening when another teacher decided to move during the break. But like most things, you get what you pay for, it seems. I took this out of my rolling briefcase each morning and put it back at the end of the day. If I was grading papers at home in the evening, it came out and was replaced before I went to bed. Within three weeks, I was running into my local office supply store hoping to find one of those generic record books with the solid cover because the cover to this was falling off at the plastic coil, and several of the pages were also beginning to separate from the coil. Before I made the complete transfer of data, I had ended up removing the coil altogether and holding the pages together with a trio of small binder clips down the edge where the coil should have been.

No instructions.  No gloves.  Nothing to put underneath the printer. No smock for you to wear.  I paid for ink and I received ink.  To my surprise, removing the seal from the bottle is the most difficult part and I was fortunately already gloved up, doing it at the kitchen sink when I discovered that it wouldn't just peel off.  Paper was already placed underneath the printer, my clothes were impervious to staining, paper towels were next to the printer, and the cap on the tank was lifted up and placed on its holding spot.  (Lift it up, doesn't screw.)  Unscrew the outer cap from the ink bottle and carefully tilt it into the tank, gently squeeze until the ink reaches the fill line.  Lift the bottle back, wipe it off, recap if there's ink remaining.  Place the cap onto the filled tank and check again for messes.  Check everything and wipe up any drips or dribbles.  Be sure to check dark areas. like where the cap was placed and the printer case.  Repeat with other tanks as needed.  Check your clothes.  Remove your gloves.  Reset the ink levels on the printer in Maintenance and test your printer, cleaning the heads if necessary and check alignment.  I've had no problem with the ink printing clearly and will repurchase without a doubt.

My initial thought was that it was due to the matte paper, however after printing a sheet, I believe it is the entire package. The finished card is flimsy and does not lay flat against a metallic surface, but is rather bumpy. I printed using a great ink jet printer that has been a workhorse with other Avery products, yet despite the ease with which the sheet was fed through, the outcome was not impressive at all. As with most cards I print, I ran a test sheet of paper prior to printing on the Avery stock, then lined it up to assure it looks as I intend. Unfortunately, the final product just looks like a cheap, unprofessional card.

With the remaining two sheets in my packet, I am going to print words in a small font in assorted colors on the cards, then cut them into individual words. These will be added to an existing kit which we use on a metallic surface for writing sentences. In small pieces, the product tends to adhere better to a surface. Not wanting to waste a thing, I also draw on the sheet margins with colored sharpies, cut them in small sections, and then have little magnetic pieces.

Edited review:
The last wall folder just fell off of the wall last night and there was barely anything in it!

They don’t hold very much weight at all. I used one for small wristlets and wallets - that one held up well. The other two I used for about 3 pairs of flip flops each.. they both fell and broke 😑. I cleaned the wall and adhered them and let them sit for a day before I put the flip flops in. 🤷🏼‍♀️

The printer seems to work just fine, but lord help you if you want to send a fax directly from the printer.  It seems to work, but all you get is a report saying nothing was transmitted.  This is after checking everything: the phone connection, the receiving fax etc.  The connection instructions say to use the cable provided with the computer, but there was no such cable in the box, so we used a regular phone cord and the printer apparently isn't happy with that.  It's a significant waste of time to try to get through to anyone at HP who knows anything.  They all have to go running to their supervisors to get simple answers to simple questions.  In my case I wanted to know the specifications for the cable so that if I had to go out and buy one, I would know what to get.  After a half hour waiting for the answer I gave up. I haven't tried sending a fax from my computer yet - I was trying to fax a legal signature page directly on the printer.  If you don't need to send faxes this is a great machine, although the instruction screen is small and not completely intuitive.

After waiting for the delivery of this talking travel translator, I now have to wait for it to actually translate. It arrived intact in a little package without much non-recyclable materials, just the disposable plastic case which is easy to open and not much larger than the actual 3/4 of a deck of cards size of the translator. Included was a sheet of paper with 705 English travel related phrases, and a second sheet of paper which is the schematics and operating manual. Both are in English, folded and double-sided. Now for a review of the product.

PROS:
A voice announces what is on a little screen and you can change the language through any or all 7 options.
With one button, you can hear it in your "Home" language.
Favorites can be saved.
It is small and lightweight.
If you study this, you can learn phrases and speak them yourself, maybe after showing the display to a person who speaks the language you want to learn.
You can enter addresses.

CONS:
If it needs to be reset or batteries fail, you lose all your addresses and saved favorites and there is no way to backup.
It is not very sturdy in the hand and the buttons often have to be depressed more than once.
Buttons are small, but the letters on the word imprints are as tiny as a tick before dinner.
Arrow buttons don't always work and sometimes simply turn off the device.
Digital display scrolls slowly with push of button alone to display the phrase.
Backlight button is exactly the reverse of the manual's description and it turns off and on with no apparent logic.
Audio quality is lousy with some phrases being unintelligible, even in my native English.
Spanish has an extra audio blip on almost all phrase endings.
Volume button on the rear feels like it doesn't click into the three levels but slides too freely.
Volume often becomes weird with a sound that lasts for minutes of translations.
You cannot see a phrase number, then enter the number. You need to scroll through each level and then you can save that phrase as favorite, then scroll through your favorites to access it again.

Observations:
There are so many phrases with negative connotations that it makes me want to stay at home with the doors locked. Examples include: 661 There is no electricity. 671 There are cockroaches in my room. 672 There is a smell of gas. 139 I have been bitten by a dog. 150 I have been attacked. 151 I have been raped. 162 I would like to speak to a woman. (There is no "I would like to speak to a man.") 171 Someone has stolen my..... (You can add another word like -- 474 Eggs, 463 Rib, 484 Pancake, 378 Whisky, 282 Two kilos of....)

This translator was a disappointment to me because I expected the "Over 4900 Travel Related Phrases" to be phrases and not single words like days of the week, months, numbers, seasons and foods. On the other hand, sports include soccer, tennis, table tennis, golf, baseball, badmitton, and nothing more. I will continue to explore the gadget in the hopes of finding it more useful, but the main impression it made on me is that in order to use this effectively, one must follow the suggestion of phrase number 705, "Slowly, please."
kind of sllow but works for me

I can't imagine paying nearly nine dollars each for six one inch binders, but according to the list price for [[ASIN:B00BX6TQ4G Samsill 1-Inch Round Ring View Speedy Spine Binders, White - 6 Pack (M18137C)]], that's the price. Six round ring binders arrived in a cardboard box with no wrapping and Samsill inserts in only one of the binders. That's fine because nothing was wasted. As for the actual binders, they look like many others with black bolts on the rear cover holding the ring bar. In addition to the exterior full sheet clear sleeves, the interior offers two clear bottom pockets. The one on the rear is embossed with the Samsill product information and Made in the USA badge. The spine actually offers the bottom pocket as well because it's been constructed with the pocket as one piece. The cardboard filler is very sturdy and the rounded corners of the binder are well done. Because the clear cover is not glossy, it's more appealing to me. These binders are guaranteed for life but I haven't investigated the return policy for faulty binders. Their single insert states that these are "ERGONOMIC, ANTIMICROBIAL, BIODEGRADABLE, and BREAST CANCER AWARENESS," the last which I find is an odd addition to the list.

The video on Amazon provided by Samsill shows a very content and thrilled woman who assembles erect binders by quickly slipping in the spine inserts. I personally am never as thrilled when a spine insert falls quickly into the spine but hope her thrill lasted longer than mine when the inserts fell out just as quickly. If one is to fold 1/4" of the paper insert as recommended, the claim of 5x faster than traditional view binders is greatly reduced. That also increases the time it takes to remove the folded insert to replace it for reuse.

Each of the six binders I received is a little wrinkly on the interior of the back cover. None is exact in size, but they are very close. Each has a very loose clear section over the white vinyl on the spine, the speedy spine. These binders are fine, better than many but not by much. I believe they are not worthy of the very high list price and therefore can only rate them as an average three stars.

I bought this product to use with Adult Coloring Books during the Pandemic as I am home bound with a medical condition. I am not pressing hard and do not have that problem with a less expensive brand! I cannot get my money back but hopefully can protect you from buying this product!

Each envelope weighs about 20 grams or .7 ounce including the removable seal cover strip. The envelopes are positioned upright, stacked side by side, so I have to be careful in removing the individual envelopes until I have used more. These machines don't hold quite as long as they used to; we've had two of them break in the last eight months, but they had both been in use for several years at that point. That said, they are the only brand we use in our accounting office...and we have one early-80s model that is still going strong!

Update: December 17, 2015 -- Changing my review from four stars to three stars. We ordered two calculators on September 25, 2014. I have one of them (and hardly ever use the tape), and the owner of our firm has the other one. He also hardly ever uses the tape, but this morning that mechanism broke and the paper will not advance. In addition, you now get an "E" in the left margin of the display and the machine will not function. Definitely not made well anymore. (The early-80s model I mentioned above is still flawless, of course. The rest of us have to replace our newer models every few years, it seems, although this one barely made it past one year, and with very light use of the paper-advance mechanism at that.)

The Wilson Jones View-Tab Transparent Dividers, Extra Wide, 5-Tab Set, Multicolor Square Tabs (W55069) are by far the best dividers I've used because they are sturdy and truly extra wide.  I use them in a binder containing paper sheets in addition to plastic protectors, and each tab is clearly visible.  I'm getting another set because I'd like to use them in a binder containing embroidery threads which I've never to be able to separate in a binder using dividers.  I've used several types of dividers lately and I have to say that for the price, these are my favorites.
This product is beautiful however, it keeps jamming.  Since it did not cost much, I will purchase another brand.

I was expecting more for a plain white binder which is priced as high as is the Wilson Jones Heavy Duty D-Ring View Binder.  These are made from polypropylene, a neutral plastic that is extremely durable, and Wilson Jones guarantees satisfaction for 1 year.  These binders have D-rings that supposedly hold up to 25% more paper than round ring binders, and last ten times longer than a comparable binder made of vinyl, but judging from the appearance, I'm not sold on this information.  The one I received is huge, as expected, but what I wasn't pleased with was the wrinkled and bubbly appearance of the exterior and interior pockets.  The pockets are substantial and cover the spine as well as the front and back, but the quality is not impressive.  Perhaps the one I received was an irregular, but if this is the standard, I'd suggest looking for another.  In its defense, the D-Ring apparatus opens and closes perfectly either by pressing the tabs or separating the rings manually.

I will rate the Canon P1-DHV-3 a weak 4 star rating, 4 for the actual calculator and a -1 due to the terrible manual. I accessed the PDF version of the single folded oversized page online at the Canon website that is printed on the box and printed a copy which looks to be a reduced version of exactly the same paper, so now I have one clean and unfolded pair of papers in addition to the folded and wrinkly large sheet.

As for the actual printing calculator, it is better then the last one I purchased, a Casio that is about 20+ years old and still functional, in that it is clean and new and close to the same size. It uses the same size rolls of paper, some things never change, but be aware that this one comes out of the box with one of the small test rolls. If you intend to print on paper, stock up because the included roll is about an inch diameter and that includes a thick cardboard core which leaves you with about 1/2 inch maximum thickness on the roll. The ink roller is a replaceable CP-16, good to know if you print a lot. Included in the box is a set of 4 AA batteries but there is no AC adapter in the box, so you'll have to purchase that separately. It plugs into the rear of the unit, behind the paper holder wire. I do not care for the wire due to its small gauge and lack of any holding contraption to keep the roll in the calculator and not unwound on the floor behind the desk if it's been knocked over. This is a pet peeve of many printing calculators for me because I've experienced the flying paper roll too many times. It seldom occurs with full rolls, but these little ones seem to grow wings as the paper is depleted and the roll becomes lighter.

Functionally, this calculator does what is described, having the currency conversion and cost-sell-margin functions escalate it to the level above basics. Because the paper roll flows so freely, I would not call it a "Handheld" printing calculator as it's described on the box, but with a little creativity, I will be fabricating something to keep the roll in place so that I can use it in the hand and not on the desk to try it out. It is described as "Desktop" on Amazon and that's what I'd call it.
The ink that came with the pen ran out with a week.

The Clear Ad acrylic magazine holder works well not only for holding magazines or documents in the office, but it is suitable to use as a recipe holder in the kitchen if you have the space. We were very disappointed with the [[ASIN:B00NCEE44O VELCRO 12" x 1/2" Bow Ties]] because they simply do not look attractive.  They are VELCRO and as a result, are quite flat. These will not replace other wrapping items in our home for gift giving, nor will they take the place of the VELCRO strips we have purchased  in bulk for cables.  Hook and loop products are terrific, I just would not recommend these ties as a decorative option, especially at more than a dollar and a half each.  Cut out construction paper sealed with a glue stick would give a similar impression as these.  Their redeeming quality is that they can be used to wrap cables instead of packages.

This is something for a yard sale/garage sale type setting. The lid still does not sit flush but the lock does engage. For the price it's not worth the return hassle bit wanted to put out a FYI for future purchasers.

This is true of all wrist rest pads. Don't use them!

ANY pressure applied to the bottom of your wrist will only make carpal tunnel syndrome worse.

Use NO pad. Keep wrist just barely elevated above tabletop. It should not touch anything. If you let the Carpal tunnel area rest on the pads, all you're doing is compressing the muscles and tendons in that area, making it even more likely to get swollen and sore. So stay away ftom these devices. The only time to use them is momentarily when you're not typing.
Yes it might feel good for a minute but then it'll get worse and you'll need the wrist rest pad more and more, until eventually you need surgery. So stay away and you'll never need surgery!

I wonder who came up with the "Organizher" name. Are "He" people prohibited from using this pad? I must admit that I thought there was a typo when I requested this item, but learned that there's an entire line Mead has come up with for "Her" people. Aside from this personal turn off mr me, here's my impression of the [[ASIN:B00GDDTZ5Y Mead Organizher To-Do Magnetic Notepad, 4 x 9 Inches, 75 Sheets Per Pad (57123)]].

This is a single pad of paper that costs about ten cents per page, or if you do tear each sheet down the middle, that's about a nickel per page. The quality of the paper is good and the lists can really be used for whatever you like. Considering acreage per page, the top embellishment reduces the amount of paper on which you can write. The lines are spaced to allow for a writing style that's not compressed and you can also write on the reverse side of the paper. The pad adhere well to a magnetic sheet like a refrigerator, and I'm sure that as the paper is used, it will remain even more steady with its reduced weight. Because the papers in this pad are perforated to allow splitting into two, I'll give this pad three stars. I do write lists, love good quality paper, pens and ink, but would not purchase this pad at its current price because there are so many other options available that are more attractive and less costly. For a price around three dollars, I would recommend it despite the name of the line.

In practical terms, for many uses I found it easier anyway to select my target area with an unaided left eye and then use my right to look at it closely, switching after the right got tired. This product is a great idea for one who reads books and may want to jot notes in a dim-lit environment.  My experience while using the first pen has been somewhat 'pretty good' in that, it lights up a whole page however, after approximately thirty minutes of use, the light in the pen gradually becomes dim.  Since quite a few of these come in a package, I use another one and have learned, when any of these become dim, click it off for a few hours for a somewhat recharge.  After it is clicked back on, the light remains bright for a shorter time than before.  Again, the price is right for the number of pens, however.

For printing photos, my preferred printers are Canon. I don't use this one or the little one I've had for a very long time for documents, though, because I have others which employ inks that aren't as costly. With Canon, I always go with their cartridges, never taking a chance with generics or off brand. The TS9020 gave me no problems during setup and since mine is the white version, I am especially careful to keep it clean. I hope the white doesn't yellow over time as many plastics tend to do, and will note that this printer is heavy on the cheaper plastic components. I tried the duplex printing to assure that it works, but won't use it often since I use the printer typically for photos. Because I like the ink quality, I also use this for inkjet label printing. I've only used this for a couple of weeks and my ink levels are still good, after printing photos on both glossy and matte photo papers. I enjoy printing wirelessly from the phone but prefer to use a computer with a larger screen and more editing options. The cost of the 6 ink cartridges is in line with other Canon cartridges, I guess, but with the greater number, the total cost is higher. Like most home printers now, all parts are delicate, so I'm cautious, even when touching the easy to read screen or using the card reader.

I currently use a magnetized pocket organizer that is about twice the size of this one on my refrigerator door, but the magnet is not very strong so it consistently slides down with every opening and closing of the door. I was intending to use the [[ASIN:B00JJRMK9Y Five Star Locker Organizer, Tri-Pocket with Dry Erase Surface, 7 x 7.5 Inches, White with Cobalt Blue Pockets (72622)]] for our front metal door, however because it is much more to my liking, this one will replace the sliding pocket on the refrigerator and the other will be donated. Our front door will remain pocket free for now.

This was made in China of very white plastic with blue accents and has a rounded symmetry, making it very attractive in person. All lines curve with the exception of the flat backing and substantial rear magnet. My only criticism is that the molded five star emblem and stars in the larger right sided pocket interfere with writing when I jot down a note with a dry erase marker. I think the current price is very high for this item, but do find it to be a good quality which is worthy of about a dozen dollars at most. If I were Five Star, I would include a marker.

I like labels and have recently received several types and sizes for review.  The [[ASIN:B00JVUQ52C Avery Kids Self-Laminating Labels, Assorted, Pack of 48 (41433)]] may very well be my favorites of right now, despite their hefty price tag.  Made in the US, each of the 4 sheets contains three sizes in two shapes and their colored tabs are attractive.  Write on the label using a permanent marker, remove the backing sheet from the laminate, press down on the laminate.  Now you have your laminated label and it should live through your dishwasher or microwave, they say.  I am reluctant to try, so I cannot tell you if this is a true statement.  I've used the skinny labels for cable identification and am prepared to use the quarter sized dots on jelly jars after using a very fine tipped sharpie.  At over $3 per sheet, these are more expensive than I would pay, but I would look for them at half that price.  They are labels.

Kai scissors are just the best. Those serrated blades cut so smooth and effortlessly. For a big treat, try a pair of the "pro" models for cutting out garments. So smooth and easy on your hands.  I have several pairs of both.

My favorite pens! Great feel and smooth flow of ink. The fact that the color gel ink matches the color of the pen is a fun touch. I use them in my home office so even pink ink is okay! The ink colors are dark enough that there is absolutely no issue seeing what you're writing. The only reason I didn't give them 5 stars is that they run out of ink faster than other pens. Good thing there are 8 to a pack!

I am not a student but love paper, pens and ink. The [[ASIN:B007CGUHHG Five Star Composition Book, 100 College-Ruled Sheets, 9.75 x 7.5 Inch Sheet Size, Lime (72861)]] is a nice carrying size, fine plastic cover, sewn binding and black tape spine reinforcement. The paper is standard for five star with the blue lines and red line down the left margin. The rounded corners are cut well.

This book is fine for pencil, ballpoint, rollerball pens, however I would not recommend it for fountain pens because most will bleed through the paper to the reverse side. Some may actually bleed onto the page beneath. I use Rhodia pads when I want a high quality paper with perforated sheets and to my surprise, some can be purchased for the same price as this one. One thing to note is that the plastic cover on this is flexible, so care must be taken if you are writing in it without resting it on a flat surface for support.

Wilson Jones hit it out of the ballpark with the View-Tab Transparent Dividers.  Sold in a package of 5 sets of 8-Tabbed dividers, it's a breeze to assemble what you need quickly.  Just use the online template to create a sheet of your tab names and print it, thereby allowing you to view each tab from the rear sheet without actually writing, printing or sticking adhesive labels on the physical dividers.  For me, the only weakness is that unless a reversed page is created for the front of the binder, the identification of the tab requires an additional step to figure out.  The curve of each tab is more comfortable for me than angular tabs and I really love being able to also have a clear view at the first page beneath each divider.  This opens the door to creativity in designing the pages.  I'm certain these will hold up well and am also impressed at the affordability when these are purchased in a package of 5.  I also really like the colors.

The boxes in which I've been storing and using hanging file folders aren't designed with lowered sides, so they haven't been the most satisfactory. I had high hopes for the [[ASIN:B00G476NR2 Bankers Box Hang'N'Stor Medium-Duty Storage Boxes, Letter, 4-Pack (0078403)]] and for the most part, they are suited to my needs. They are easy to assemble whether or not you view the diagram printed on one one of the hidden folds if you are intuitive or have ever assembled a Bankers type box. I had no problem with the medium weight cardboard tearing or not folding along the fold lines. It took less than a minute to put one together and it looks good. The "Fastfold" technique truly is really quick. One thing I'd suggest is to fold in the hand opening prior to folding the larger sides over inside the box. That was my only burp with the first box. I assembled a lid as soon as I removed it from the top of the flat package and didn't realize at first that the lids are designed with a hand flap that slides into the carrying cutouts on the box for additional support. I initially folded the flap into the lid ends prior to constructing the lid ends. There is no additional material included in the package, with the exception of a little orange sweepstakes entry attached onto the rear interior of my package.

I've owned and used assorted Bankers Boxes throughout many years including those with lift off lids, fixed lids, and drawers. This is my first for hanging files. I am disappointed in the smoothness by which the brand new hanging files I'm using move to and fro. Because the box is medium weight, not extremely durable and there is no additional reinforcement or railing, I don't think they will hold up to daily use. I'd recommend these attractive boxes for storage with occasional file movement. On the other hand, because they are lighter in weight without any reinforcement, it's easier to lift the boxes for transporting them. I'm not expecting these boxes to last forever, but for now they are fine and I can recommend them for cases where hanging folders are needed. For other uses, I'd suggest using boxes without the shorter sides, particularly the heavier weight boxes.
Easy to put together and looks great

Got these for my parents and for my at home office needs. Kind of disappointed with this shipment of tape corrector. They did not seem to work and would NOT roll correctly. Very frustrating. Had to throw some of them away since they were such a mess.
Garbage. It didn't even power on.
I sent these back to replace them with tags that included strings on them.  My mistake.  They are sturdy and a good size for my craft business

With only one review of 5 stars I thought it would be a good way to file and organize my papers. Wrong! The folder itself is like an accordion but it does not stay open to get the papers out and in. The inner file folders in various colors is a good thing except that they are extremely thin. I tried it to see if I could make it usable but there’s no way I could get more than a few papers in one slot. The description says it’s a ‘large capacity’ organizer but it isn’t. It’s also said that it can be used with ‘legal’ size papers - this is something I can’t imagine unless you actually fold the document in half. I only received it yesterday and it’s going right back - there are much better ones to choose from and that have a decent price as well.

Yes, the Avery labels print to edge, but only if you successfully align the sheets in your printer and have adjusted the template document you are printing.  I used the Avery on line program and had a lot of difficulty getting it right, but fortunately, printed on plain paper prior to inserting the label sheets.  The rustic Kraft Brown paper is good for a number of purposes, is too plain for others.  the scalloped oval shape is similar to others I've used but they look totally different than those.  These are not removable labels, so to speak, so once you apply them, that's where they will stay.

I'm amazed that cardboard boxes can cost so much, but I've found that when I want to protect the goods I'm storing or moving, I'm constantly impressed by SmoothMove products.  The [[ASIN:B007XPBW3E Bankers Box SmoothMove Picture/Mirror Box, Medium, 37 x 4 x 27 Inches, 4 Pack (7711201)]] is identified as containing fragile items, so if others are moving your things, they hopefully will treat your pictures or mirrors with special care.  I used a sturdy tape when constructing the boxes and think that the cardboard will hold up to more than one move after being refolded.  I retained the shipping box because that's also usable for other things although it's not adjustable like the four specially picture/mirror boxes.  Love SmoothMove.
I checked the specs and it claimed that it fit my printer. Well, it didn't. I even took it to Office Depot and the guy said that it says it fit, but clearly was wrong

To my surprise, we are using this Five Star very tall stand that is designed to be used in a school locker since it was retired from the school lockers which are on a hiatus.  It is sturdy enough to stand on its own but will not hold as much weight as when it is reinforced by the sides of a locker.  The plastic drawer slides smoothly, stopping at the rear and front so it can't be pulled out of the side rails unless you want it removed.  It looks just like the photo provided and can be folded flat if not being used, allowing for storage ease.

I thought this was lomger.
Its even shorter than my badge so u cant see the 'nursing student' sticking out at the bottom.
Utterly useless. And shipping took forever.
I was looking for an autograph pen, but this has a bit too wide a line.

The [[ASIN:B004MTDFHA Parker IM Black Gold Trim Fountain Pen, Medium Nib - 1760800]] is  a nice looking Parker pen which arrives in a soft box containing a single cartridge and warranty.  If there's a problem with the pen,  Parker requires a copy of your sales order because Amazon does not stamp the date of purchase on the warranty.  With this pen, use of the warranty is likely.  I'd read reviews abound with complaints regarding the faulty ink flow to the nib and the one I received is awful.  I'd also purchased an ink converter, so I know it's not the cartridge, as I've tried about a half dozen different inks.  With each, even if I leave the pen for an hour, it dries and I must either prime the converter or dip the nib into water or ink.  I was embarrassed at a meeting when the pen would not write and  I needed to sign a document.  Visually, this pen is five star.  In the hand, it's also five star.  If it worked properly, it would be a total five star value.  Unfortunately, a pen that doesn't write is worthless unless you've purchased it for another purpose.  I purchase pens to use as a tool for transporting ink to paper, so this one is a great disappointment.  I'd had a very old Parker fountain pen stolen inside of a bag and really wanted another, but this one will not replace the great pen I'd had for about forty years.  I cannot recommend this pen, particularly since there are so many really good fountain pens available currently.

I've been using the 3M Paper Cutter for about a week now and I'm very pleased with the purchase.  It stands nicely in a pen cup which makes for the easiest storage and reduced chance of misplacing the tool.  Although it sometimes takes a little work to get it started, for the most part, I have just begun with a slight push or tug and it's moved right along, slicing open the envelope, bag, package tape, or print article.  It does not work for anything heftier than perhaps a construction paper weight, but that seems obvious because of the lightweight nature of the plastic handle and hidden cutter.  I don't know how long this will last, but for now, it fulfills it's purpose.  It is not a pair of scissors, knife or box cutter, but for lightweight applications, it's better than satisfactory.  Not a bad tool for an arthritic hand, I would imagine.
Just beautiful vintage note pads. I know my brother will love them!
Save your money. This moves around too much to be very useful. It slides off the keyboard tray. The two strips that are supposed to keep it in place need to be 3-4 times the size.
Garbage. Turned in 2 days and charms fell off
Cute bags but no padding.
I ordered several pkgs of these to keep my office/storage room tidy.  They do hold a lot of stuff and I wrapped packaging tape all around them, so they don't come apart.
These are ok but do not take a full half sheet of 8.5x11 paper, which I wanted.  Oh well, cannot seem to find other sheets in the size I want, so making do with these, which are durable and clear.

I bought this to attend an outdoor theater production of A Midsummer Night’s Dream. The play was 100 minutes long. The cushion was only helpful for about the first 50-60 minutes. After that, I was pretty uncomfortable. This was much better than the seat cushions that my sister had bought.  It is nice and light and comes with a handle which I am thankful for because there is no parking in downtown and we had to walk about six blocks in order to get to where we were going.  For the price, it was a decent deal.
it was very hard to bear down. i don't think i will be using these pens
very cute but when you tear off a sheet from the pad - it does not tear clean, therefore the glue on the backside of your sheet is still sticky = not very practical - not very usable
Doesn't feel like a soft #1 pencil at all -- more like a #2.  It's quite disappointing.

This is a neat little nostalgia piece I'd give it a five-star cuz I'm a huge Zelda fan but the problem is it leans kind of forward, not a deal breaker. It does open letters like it says it does so there's not much to complain about on that front.

The Wilson Jones Cut and Sewn Binder, Round Rings, 1" in Navy Blue is attractive and easily holds a hundred sheets of 20 lb weight filler paper with 16 dividers, maybe more in yours.  The contrasting interior and stitching gives a classy appearance and the elastic sewn in strap enables me to hold loose papers if I'm careful.  I have inserted a pen into the side flap, so I always have a writing instrument at hand.  The rings are easy to open and shut and the large interior pocket is useful as well.  My only concern with this is the attached CA PROP 65 WARNING which states, "This product contains chemicals known to the State of California to cause birth defects or other reproductive harm."  Don't purchase this if you are looking for something in which to hold your prenatal papers!

Actual staples are a classic for a reason.  This doesn't keep the papers together well.  It's fine unless you page through or read through your "stapled" papers.  Then it falls apart.  A paper clip would be better.  Easy to use and cute though.

As of today, Avery.com/print offers 111 templates which can be used for these dissolvable labels, but you can simply write on them with a pencil or pen.  I like to print my labels, particularly if I'm presenting a canned good as a gift.  Like most other Avery labels, these have a code printed on the package, but there is no clear date of manufacturing, so you would have to contact Avery to determine their coding.  I don't expect to need that information because I'm using these labels right away.  Before I applied one onto a jar, I felt compelled to test what is meant by "dissolvable."  I removed one corner from one of the five sheets of labels and stuck it on a jar.  I then ran the jar under cool water and to my surprise and delight, the label really and truly dissolved underneath my fingers.  It washed away without the use of a sponge, soap or even warm water.  It felt sort of slimy and was then gone!  Now these labels look like none other I've used.  They are not a true bright white, but they are whitish.  Printing on these is exactly like my other Avery labels and because they are fresh, the printing was clear and remained clear.  I've experienced printing that just did not take on old labels, so I am careful about using fresh labels now.  If you do not need to use FDA approved adhesive safe for indirect food contact and labels which dissolve in water, you do not need these labels.  They are not cheap and they will dissolve if you put them on an envelope and they encounter rain in their delivery to the recipient.  These are perfect for canning or food storage purposes and that's exactly what I'm doing with this package of 90.  They were made in Mexico, south of the border.
nice one !
We had this shredder for a year and I loved it.  Small, compact, easy to use, seemingly easy to maintain.  But then it quit working.  We had only filled up the bin twice.  Major bummer.
The pen finish is good, but it is too heavy and the cap will pop off unexpectedly.  The cap does not stay on securely..
What good is it to purchase tags for a laser printer when you do not offer a template??? Very disappointed! This was what sold me- you could use them on a laser printer! Waste of money

We needed about 150 tickets and found these for a very reasonable price on Amazon. Our order consisted of all white single tickets which was better than fine for our needs. The single tickets were perfect for entry to our event. Nobody had a problem tearing off tickets from their rolls and we had no difficulties whatsoever. I'd buy these again.
